taskCaption: cube ends up inside the tray (terminal-relation-only diagnostic)

pilotMetadata.tests: Asserts, via the FROZEN matcher, ONLY that the cube's TERMINAL relation to the tray is INSIDE (one hard OBJECT_RELATION_GOAL -> goal_satisfaction). It does NOT require an initial NEAR state, a NEAR->INSIDE transition, a CONTAINMENT_CHANGE event, contact, hand, or temporal order. The camera analogue of the RLBench value-only target: it answers 'did the cube end inside?' and nothing more.
pilotMetadata.enforcedVsAuthoring: ENFORCED: terminal INSIDE(cube,tray) only (goal_satisfaction reads the robot's terminal relation in csg.matcher._goal_satisfaction_distance). AUTHORING ONLY (not verifier-enforced): geometry source/category, confidences. A 'born-inside' episode (cube inside the whole time, but it MOVES so the extractor reports a terminal INSIDE) PASSes this target -- which is exactly why the strictly-stronger object_inside_container_relation_event target exists.
pilotMetadata.deferredNote: Marker-only 3A has no honest evidence for who/what moved the cube; this target deliberately asserts the weakest honest claim (terminal containment) so the relation-event target can be shown strictly stronger.
pilotMetadata.notAGoldTask: Pilot diagnostic, deliberately NOT added to gold_tests/. Read it for terminal-containment mappability, not as a full object_inside_container acceptance test.
